metadata.title: Ancient Prayers of the Sacred Traditions
metadata.description: A collection of prayers from Torah, Bible, Quran, Papyrus of Ani, and African spiritual traditions with meanings and origins

prayers.title: The Lord's Prayer
prayers.origin: Gospel of Matthew 6:9-13, Gospel of Luke 11:2-4
prayers.taught_by: Yeshua Ha'Mashiach (Jesus Christ) to his disciples on the Mount

languages.english:
Our Father, who art in heaven,
Hallowed be Thy name.
Thy kingdom come,
Thy will be done,
On earth as it is in heaven.
Give us this day our daily bread,
And forgive us our trespasses,
As we forgive those who trespass against us.
And lead us not into temptation,
But deliver us from evil.
For Thine is the kingdom, the power, and the glory,
Forever and ever.
Amen.
languages.shona:
Baba vedu muri kudenga,
Zita renyu ngarikudzwe.
Umambo hwenyu ngahuuye,
Kuda kwenyu ngakuitwe,
Panyika sezvakunoitwa kudenga.
Tipei nhasi chingwa chedu chamazuva ose.
Tikanganwirei zvatakakutadzirai,
Sezvatinokanganwirawo vanotitadzira.
Musatitungamirire mukuedzwa,
Asi mutinunure kubva kune zvakaipa.
Nokuti umambo ndohwenyu, nesimba, nokubwinya,
Nokusingaperi.
Ameni.
languages.aramaic_transliteration:
Abwoon d'bwashmaya,
Nethqadash shmakh.
Teytey malkuthakh.
Nehwey tzevyanakh aykanna d'bwashmaya aph b'arha.
Hawvlan lachma d'sunqanan yaomana.
Washboqlan khaubayn aykana daph khnan shbwoqan l'khayyabayn.
Wela tahlan l'nesyuna ela patzan min bisha.
Metol dilakhie malkutha wahayla wateshbukhta l'ahlam almin.
Amen.

prayers.meaning: This prayer acknowledges the Heavenly Father, seeks alignment with divine will, asks for daily sustenance, forgiveness, and protection from spiritual harm. It affirms faith in God's eternal kingdom and power.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The foundational prayer of Christianity, teaching humility, forgiveness, and dependence on the Divine. Each line corresponds to a spiritual principle: acknowledgment of Source, surrender to divine will, trust in provision, the law of forgiveness, and protection through faith.

prayers.title: Shema Yisrael
prayers.taught_by: Moses (Musa) to the children of Israel

languages.hebrew:
שְׁמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ יְהוָה אֶחָד
וְאָהַבְתָּ אֵת יְהוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ בְּכָל לְבָבְךָ וּבְכָל נַפְשְׁךָ וּבְכָל מְאֹדֶךָ
languages.transliteration:
Shema Yisrael, Adonai Eloheinu, Adonai Echad.
V'ahavta et Adonai Elohekha b'khol l'vavkha uv'khol nafsh'kha uv'khol m'odekha.
languages.english:
Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is One.
And you shall love the LORD your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your might.

prayers.meaning: The foundational declaration of Jewish faith, affirming the oneness of God and commanding total love and devotion to the Creator.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This is the central prayer of Judaism, recited morning and evening. It represents the covenant between God and Israel, declaring monotheism and complete devotion.

prayers.title: Al-Fatiha (The Opening)
prayers.taught_by: Revealed to Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) through Angel Jibreel

languages.arabic:
بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ
الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ
مَالِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّينِ
إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِينُ
اهْدِنَا الصِّرَاطَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ
صِرَاطَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ
غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّينَ
languages.transliteration:
Bismillahir Rahmanir Raheem
Alhamdu lillahi Rabbil 'aalameen
Ar-Rahmanir-Raheem
Maaliki Yawmid-Deen
Iyyaaka na'budu wa iyyaaka nasta'een
Ihdinas-Siraatal-Mustaqeem
Siraatal-lazeena an'amta 'alaihim
Ghayril-maghdoobi 'alaihim wa lad-daalleen
languages.english:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
All praise is due to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.
The Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
Master of the Day of Judgment.
You alone we worship, and You alone we ask for help.
Guide us on the Straight Path.
The path of those who have received Your grace;
Not the path of those who have brought down wrath upon themselves, nor of those who have gone astray.

prayers.meaning: The opening chapter of the Quran, containing the essence of Islamic faith: praise of Allah, acknowledgment of His lordship over all creation, and supplication for divine guidance.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Recited in every unit of the five daily prayers, Al-Fatiha is considered the greatest surah of the Quran. It encompasses praise, worship, and supplication in perfect balance.

prayers.title: Hymn to Ra at Dawn

languages.english:
Homage to thee, O Ra, at thy tremendous rising!
Thou risest! Thou shinest! The heavens are rolled aside!
Thou art the King of Gods, thou art the All-comprising,
From thee we come, in thee are deified.

Thy priests go forth at dawn; they wash their hearts with laughter;
Divine winds move in music across thy golden strings.
At Sunset they cry 'Hail!' In Douat thou goest after:
Thou passest through the worlds of gloom and silent things.

All faces bow before thee, all souls with worshipful chorus;
The light streams of thy rising is breath upon our lips.
Thou art the Lord of Heaven, the Lord of Earth, O Horus,
Who risest in the East and all the world eclipse!
languages.ancient_egyptian_transliteration:
Inu-k Ra em weben-k asha!
Weben-k! Hedj-k! Pet shet!
Ntk neb netjeru, ntk tem
Per-n em-k, netjer-n em-k.

prayers.meaning: A prayer of adoration to Ra, the Sun God, acknowledging his supreme power as creator and sustainer of all life. The prayer celebrates the daily resurrection of the sun as a symbol of eternal renewal.
prayers.spiritual_significance: In Egyptian cosmology, Ra's daily journey represents the cycle of death and rebirth. This hymn was recited at dawn to invoke protection, blessing, and alignment with cosmic order (Ma'at).

prayers.title: The Negative Confession (Declaration of Innocence)

languages.english:
Hail to thee, great God, Lord of the Two Truths!
I have come unto thee, my Lord, that thou mayest bring me to see thy beauty.
I know thee, I know thy name, I know the names of the Forty-Two Gods.

I have not done iniquity.
I have not robbed with violence.
I have not stolen.
I have not slain men and women.
I have not stolen grain.
I have not purloined offerings.
I have not stolen the property of the gods.
I have not uttered lies.
I have not carried away food.
I have not uttered curses.
I have not committed adultery.
I have not lain with men.
I have made none to weep.
I have not eaten the heart (worried uselessly).
I have not attacked any man.
I am not a man of deceit.
I have not stolen cultivated land.
I have not been an eavesdropper.
I have slandered no man.
I have not been angry without just cause.
I have not debauched the wife of any man.
I have not polluted myself.
I have not terrorized anyone.
I have not done that which is abominable.
I have not struck anyone.
I have not been hot-tempered.
I have not turned deaf to the words of truth.
I have not caused grief.
I have not done violence.
I have not acted hastily or without thought.
I have not stopped my ears to the words of Right and Truth.
I have not worked grief.
I have not acted with insolence.
I have not judged hastily.
I have not multiplied my words exceedingly.
I have not done harm or evil.
I have not cursed the King.
I have not fouled the water.
I have not been proud.
I have not cursed the gods.
I have not stolen.
I have not defrauded the offerings of the gods.
languages.shona:
Ndakakwazisa iwe, Mwari mukuru, Ishe weMaviri eChokwadi!
Ndauya kwauri, Ishe wangu, kuti undiise kuona runako rwako.
Ndinokuziva, ndinoziva zita rako, ndinoziva mazita eVamwari Makumi Mana neVaviri.

Handina kuita zvakaipa.
Handina kuba nesimba.
Handina kuba.
Handina kuuraya varume nevakadzi.
Handina kuba zviyo.
Handina kutora zvipo.
Handina kuba pfuma yevamwari.
Handina kutaura nhema.
Handina kubvisa chokudya.
Handina kutuka.
Handina kuita upombwe.
Handina kuita kuti mumwe acheme.
Handina kudya moyo (kunetseka pasina).
Handina kurwisa munhu.
Handisi munhu wenhema.
Handina kuba ivhu rakarimwa.
Handina kuva muteereri.
Handina kuchera munhu.
Handina kutsamwa pasina chikonzero.
Handina kusvibisa ndirini.
Handina kutyisidzira munhu.
Handina kuita zvinonyangadza.
Handina kurova munhu.
Handina kuva nehasha.
Handina kusanzwa mashoko echokwadi.
Handina kuunza shungu.
Handina kuita simba.
Handina kuita nokukurumidza.
Handina kuvhara nzeve dzangu kumashoko eChokwadi.
Handina kuita shungu.
Handina kuita namanyawi.
Handina kutonga nokukurumidza.
Handina kuwanza mashoko angu kwazvo.
Handina kuita zvakaipa kana chakaipa.
Handina kutuka Mambo.
Handina kusvibisa mvura.
Handina kuzvikudza.
Handina kutuka vamwari.
Handina kuba.
Handina kunyengera zvipo zvevamwari.

prayers.meaning: This is the declaration spoken by the soul before the 42 judges in the Hall of Two Truths (Ma'at). The deceased affirms they have lived according to cosmic law and ethical principles.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The Negative Confession represents the Egyptian concept of moral accountability. Each denial corresponds to a specific cosmic judge. Speaking truthfully was essential for the heart to balance against the Feather of Ma'at.

prayers.title: Prayer for the Heart (Heart Scarab Spell)

languages.english:
O my heart which I had from my mother!
O my heart which I had from my mother!
O my heart of my different ages!
Do not stand up as a witness against me,
Do not be opposed to me in the tribunal,
Do not be hostile to me in the presence of the Keeper of the Balance,
For you are my Ka which was in my body,
The protector who made my members hale.
Go forth to the happy place whereto we speed!
Do not make my name stink with the Entourage who make men.
Do not tell lies against me in the presence of the god!
It is indeed well that you should hear!
languages.ancient_egyptian_transliteration:
Ib-i n mut-i!
Ib-i n mut-i!
Ib-i n kheperu-i!
M aHa-k r-i m meteret,
M khesef-i m djadjat,
M neh-i m bah mekha,
Ntk ka-i imyt khat-i,
Seneb-ti irw awt-i.
Per-k r bw nefer peru-n im,
M seref ren-i m sheniu irw rekhyt.
M djed grg r-i m bah netjer!
Nefer gert sedjem-k!

prayers.meaning: A prayer to one's own heart, asking it not to testify against the soul during the weighing ceremony. The heart was considered the seat of consciousness and memory of all deeds.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This spell was inscribed on heart scarabs placed on mummies. The heart carried the record of all actions. This prayer reveals the Egyptian understanding that we cannot hide from our own conscience.

prayers.title: Opening of the Mouth Invocation

languages.english:
My mouth is opened by Ptah,
My mouth's bonds are loosed by my city-god.
Thoth has come fully equipped with spells,
He looses the bonds of Seth from my mouth.
Atum has given me my hands,
They are placed as guardians.

My mouth is given to me,
My mouth is opened by Ptah,
With that chisel of metal
With which he opened the mouth of the gods.
I am Sekhmet-Wadjet who dwells in the west of heaven,
I am Sahyt among the souls of On.

As for any spell and all words which may be spoken against me,
The gods will rise up against them,
The entire Ennead, the entire Ennead!

prayers.meaning: This ritual invocation restores the ability to speak, breathe, eat, and interact in the afterlife. It symbolizes the resurrection of all faculties needed for eternal existence.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The Opening of the Mouth was one of the most important funeral rites. It represents the activation of the spiritual body (sahu) and the restoration of all senses for the journey through Duat.

prayers.title: Hymn to Osiris

languages.english:
Glory be to thee, O Osiris Un-Nefer, the great god within Abydos,
King of eternity, Lord of everlasting,
Who passeth through millions of years in thy existence.
Thou art the eldest son of the womb of Nut,
Thou wast engendered by Geb, the Ancestor.
Thou art the lord of the crowns of the North and of the South,
Thou art the lord of the lofty white crown.
As Prince of gods and of men,
Thou hast received the crook and the flail and the dignity of thy divine fathers.

Let thy heart which is in the mountain of Amentet be content,
For thy son Horus is established upon thy throne.
Thou art crowned lord of Tattu and ruler in Abydos.
Through thee the world waxeth green in triumph
Before the might of Neb-er-tcher.

He leadeth in his train that which is and that which is not yet,
In his name of 'Ta-her-sta-nef';
He toweth along the earth by Ma'at
In his name of 'Seker';
He is exceedingly mighty and most terrible
In his name of 'Osiris';
He endureth for ever and ever
In his name of 'Un-Nefer'.

prayers.meaning: A hymn of praise to Osiris, Lord of the Underworld, celebrating his triumph over death and his eternal reign. Osiris represents the promise of resurrection for all who follow Ma'at.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Osiris's death and resurrection prefigures the journey every soul must make. His green flesh symbolizes rebirth and vegetation. Through identification with Osiris, the deceased hoped to share in his resurrection.

prayers.title: Prayer for Protection Against Serpents

languages.english:
O Rerek serpent, take thyself off, for Geb protects me!
Get up, for you have eaten a mouse, which Ra detests,
And you have chewed the bones of a putrid cat.

O serpent, I am the flame which shines on the brows of the Chaos-gods,
The flame which prepares the way for the years.
Be far removed from me, for I am Ma'at!

The serpent is Apep, come forth with his strength:
He has no power over me.
I am Set whom none can control,
Who slays the serpent at the prow of the Boat of Ra,
And sends the enemy backwards!

prayers.meaning: A protective spell against the serpent Apep (chaos) and all dangerous creatures of the underworld. It invokes divine protection through identification with the gods.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Serpents represented chaos and the enemies of cosmic order. This spell gave the deceased power over the dangers of the underworld journey, symbolizing triumph over fear and evil.

prayers.title: The Aaronic Blessing (Priestly Blessing)
prayers.taught_by: Given by YHWH to Moses for Aaron and his sons

languages.hebrew:
יְבָרֶכְךָ יְהוָה וְיִשְׁמְרֶךָ
יָאֵר יְהוָה פָּנָיו אֵלֶיךָ וִיחֻנֶּךָּ
יִשָּׂא יְהוָה פָּנָיו אֵלֶיךָ וְיָשֵׂם לְךָ שָׁלוֹם
languages.transliteration:
Y'varechecha Adonai v'yishm'recha.
Ya'er Adonai panav elecha vichuneka.
Yisa Adonai panav elecha v'yasem l'cha shalom.

prayers.meaning: A three-fold blessing invoking God's protection, favor, grace, and peace upon the people.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This is the oldest known biblical text found in the Silver Scrolls (7th century BCE). It represents the threefold nature of divine blessing: material protection, spiritual illumination, and complete peace (shalom).

prayers.title: Prayer of Jabez

languages.hebrew: אִם־בָּרֵךְ תְּבָרְכֵנִי וְהִרְבִּיתָ אֶת־גְּבוּלִי וְהָיְתָה יָדְךָ עִמִּי וְעָשִׂיתָ מֵרָעָה לְבִלְתִּי עָצְבִּי
languages.english:
Oh, that You would bless me indeed,
And enlarge my territory,
That Your hand would be with me,
And that You would keep me from evil,
That I may not cause pain!
languages.shona:
Haiwa, kana ukanduropafadza zvechokwadi,
Nokuwedzera nyika yangu,
Kuti ruoko rwako ruve neni,
Uye kuti undichengetedze kubva kuzvakaipa,
Kuti ndirege kurwadziwa!

prayers.meaning: A prayer for blessing, expansion, divine presence, and protection from causing or experiencing harm.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Jabez means 'pain' - he was named for the sorrow of his birth. Yet he prayed for blessing that would overcome his painful beginning. This teaches that our past does not determine our future.

prayers.title: Prayer of Solomon for Wisdom

languages.english:
O LORD my God, You have made Your servant king instead of my father David,
But I am but a little child; I do not know how to go out or come in.
And Your servant is in the midst of Your people whom You have chosen,
A great people, too numerous to be numbered or counted.
Therefore give to Your servant an understanding heart
To judge Your people, that I may discern between good and evil.
For who is able to judge this great people of Yours?
languages.shona:
Haiwa ISHE Mwari wangu, makaita muranda wenyu mambo pachinzvimbo chababa vangu Dhavhidhi,
Asi ini ndiri mwana muduku; handizivi kuenda nokubuda.
Uye muranda wenyu ari pakati pevanhu venyu vamakasarudza,
Vanhu vazhinji, vakawanda zvokuti havagoni kuverengwa kana kunyorwa.
Naizvozvo ipai muranda wenyu mwoyo unonzwisisa
Kuti atongere vanhu venyu, kuti ndigone kusiyanisa pakati pezvakanaka nezvakaipa.
Nokuti ndiani anogona kutonga vanhu venyu ava vakuru?

prayers.meaning: Solomon's humble prayer asking not for wealth or long life but for wisdom to lead God's people justly.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This prayer pleased God because Solomon sought the welfare of others above personal gain. It teaches that wisdom is the highest gift to seek, for with it comes all other blessings.

prayers.title: Prayer of Daniel

languages.english:
O Lord, great and awesome God, who keeps His covenant and mercy with those who love Him,
And with those who keep His commandments,
We have sinned and committed iniquity, we have done wickedly and rebelled,
Even by departing from Your precepts and Your judgments.

O Lord, righteousness belongs to You, but to us shame of face,
To our kings, our princes, and our fathers,
Because we have sinned against You.
To the Lord our God belong mercy and forgiveness,
Though we have rebelled against Him.

O Lord, hear! O Lord, forgive! O Lord, listen and act!
Do not delay for Your own sake, my God,
For Your city and Your people are called by Your name.

prayers.meaning: A prayer of national confession and repentance, acknowledging sins while appealing to God's mercy and covenant faithfulness.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Daniel prayed this while in Babylonian exile, interceding for his people. It models corporate repentance and faith in God's promises despite present circumstances.

prayers.title: Psalm 23 (The Shepherd's Psalm)
prayers.meaning: A psalm of absolute trust in God as protector, provider, and guide through all of life's challenges, even death itself.
prayers.spiritual_significance: David wrote from experience as both a shepherd and a king who faced many dangers. The imagery of shepherd, table, and anointing represents God's complete care - protection, provision, and honor.

prayers.title: Ayat al-Kursi (The Throne Verse)

languages.arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ
languages.transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illa Huwal-Hayyul-Qayyum. La ta'khuzuhu sinatun wa la nawm. Lahu ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ard. Man zal-lazi yashfa'u 'indahu illa bi-iznih. Ya'lamu ma bayna aydihim wa ma khalfahum. Wa la yuhituna bi shay'im-min 'ilmihi illa bima sha'a. Wasi'a kursiyyuhus-samawati wal-ard. Wa la ya'uduhu hifzuhuma. Wa Huwal-'Aliyyul-'Azim.
languages.english:
Allah! There is no god but He, the Living, the Self-subsisting, Eternal.
No slumber can seize Him nor sleep.
His are all things in the heavens and on earth.
Who is there that can intercede in His presence except as He permits?
He knows what is before them and what is behind them.
Nor shall they compass any of His knowledge except as He wills.
His Throne extends over the heavens and the earth,
And He feels no fatigue in guarding and preserving them.
For He is the Most High, the Supreme.

prayers.meaning: The greatest verse of the Quran, describing Allah's absolute sovereignty, omniscience, and omnipotence. His Throne (Kursi) encompasses all creation.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) called this the greatest verse in the Quran. Reciting it provides protection and brings the reciter closer to paradise. It summarizes the essence of Tawhid (Divine Unity).

prayers.title: The Jesus Prayer (Prayer of the Heart)
prayers.origin: Based on Luke 18:13 and the Desert Fathers tradition
prayers.taught_by: Desert Fathers of Egypt (3rd-4th century CE)

languages.greek: Κύριε Ἰησοῦ Χριστέ, Υἱὲ τοῦ Θεοῦ, ἐλέησόν με τὸν ἁμαρτωλόν
languages.transliteration: Kyrie Iesou Christe, Yie tou Theou, eleison me ton hamartolon
languages.english: Lord Jesus Christ, Son of God, have mercy on me, a sinner.
languages.shona: Ishe Jesu Kristu, Mwanakomana waMwari, ndinzwirei ngoni, mutadzi.

prayers.meaning: A short prayer of continuous remembrance, combining faith in Christ's divinity with humble acknowledgment of human sinfulness.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This prayer is meant to be repeated continuously, synchronized with breathing, until it becomes as natural as the heartbeat. It represents hesychasm - the practice of inner stillness and union with God.

prayers.title: Prayer to the Four Winds

languages.english:
O Atum who art in the great Waters!
Grant that I may have mastery in the sky,
That I may have power on earth,
That I may have power in the waters.

Hail to you, O North Wind, coming from the Mediterranean,
Who embraces all, who art in the breeze,
May you give me life!

Hail to you, O South Wind, coming from the Land of Nubia,
Who brings the inundation, who makes all things green,
May you give me power!

Hail to you, O East Wind, coming from the Land of Sunrise,
Who opens the windows of heaven,
May you give me light!

Hail to you, O West Wind, coming from the Land of Sunset,
Who guards the gates of the Duat,
May you give me peace!

prayers.meaning: An invocation to the four cardinal winds, each associated with different divine qualities and blessings needed for the afterlife journey.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The four winds represent the complete coverage of divine protection and blessing from all directions. This prayer establishes the deceased's relationship with all cosmic forces.

prayers.title: Prayer Before the Seven Gates

languages.english:
O you who guard the First Gate,
Whose name is 'She whose face is turned backward, whose hands are many,'
I have come to you and I know you,
I know your name and the name of the god who guards you.
Open to me, for I am the Ba which exists among the followers of Osiris!

O Doorkeeper of the Second Gate,
I have made my way,
I know you, I know your name.
I am one who passes by you in peace,
For I am equipped with the words of power.
I have come with Ma'at on my lips and truth in my heart.

Grant me passage through all Seven Gates,
That I may reach the Hall of Osiris,
That I may stand in the presence of the Great God,
That I may receive offerings among the blessed dead,
That I may live forever in the Field of Reeds!

prayers.meaning: A prayer requesting passage through the seven gates of the underworld, each guarded by a deity who must be addressed by name.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Knowledge was power in Egyptian afterlife beliefs. Knowing the names of guardians granted passage. This reflects the idea that spiritual preparation in life enables safe passage through death.

prayers.title: Gayatri Mantra

languages.sanskrit:
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः
तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्
languages.transliteration:
Om Bhur Bhuvaḥ Swaḥ
Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi
Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat
languages.english:
Om, we meditate on the glory of that Being
Who has created this universe,
Who is worthy of worship,
Who is the embodiment of Knowledge and Light,
Who is the remover of all Sin and Ignorance.
May He enlighten our Intellect.

prayers.meaning: A prayer to the Supreme Being (Savitar/Sun) for divine illumination and guidance of the intellect toward righteousness.
prayers.spiritual_significance: One of the oldest known prayers, the Gayatri is considered the essence of all Vedas. It is chanted at dawn, noon, and dusk. Its three lines correspond to the three worlds: physical, mental, and spiritual.

prayers.title: Mahamrityunjaya Mantra (Death-Conquering Prayer)

languages.sanskrit:
ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्
उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्मृत्योर्मुक्षीय माऽमृतात्
languages.transliteration:
Om Tryambakam Yajamahe
Sugandhim Pushti-Vardhanam
Urvarukamiva Bandhanan
Mrityor Mukshiya Maamritat
languages.english:
We worship the Three-eyed One (Lord Shiva),
Who is fragrant and who nourishes all beings.
May He liberate us from death,
For the sake of Immortality,
As the cucumber is severed from its bondage to the vine.

prayers.meaning: A prayer to Lord Shiva for liberation from death, disease, and the cycle of rebirth, seeking immortality of the soul.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Known as the 'Great Death-Conquering Mantra,' it is chanted for healing, protection, and spiritual liberation. The three eyes represent sun, moon, and fire - past, present, and future.

prayers.title: Buddhist Refuge Prayer

languages.pali:
Buddhaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi
Dhammaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi
Saṅghaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi
languages.english:
I take refuge in the Buddha.
I take refuge in the Dharma (the Teaching).
I take refuge in the Sangha (the Community).

prayers.meaning: The fundamental Buddhist declaration of faith, taking shelter in the Three Jewels: the Awakened One, the Truth, and the Community of practitioners.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This is the formal commitment to the Buddhist path. The three refuges represent the enlightened teacher, the liberating truth, and the supportive community - all necessary for spiritual awakening.

prayers.title: Metta Sutta (Loving-Kindness Prayer)

languages.pali:
Sabbe sattā sukhi hontu
Sabbe sattā averā hontu
Sabbe sattā abyāpajjhā hontu
Sabbe sattā anīghā hontu
Sabbe sattā sukhī attānaṃ pariharantu
languages.english:
May all beings be happy.
May all beings be free from hostility.
May all beings be free from ill-will.
May all beings be free from anxiety.
May all beings take care of themselves with ease.

prayers.meaning: A prayer radiating unconditional love and goodwill to all sentient beings without exception.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Metta (loving-kindness) meditation is one of the four Brahmaviharas (divine abodes). It dissolves barriers between self and others, cultivating universal compassion.

prayers.title: Tashlich Prayer

languages.hebrew: מִי־אֵל כָּמוֹךָ נֹשֵׂא עָוֹן וְעֹבֵר עַל־פֶּשַׁע לִשְׁאֵרִית נַחֲלָתוֹ לֹא־הֶחֱזִיק לָעַד אַפּוֹ כִּי־חָפֵץ חֶסֶד הוּא יָשׁוּב יְרַחֲמֵנוּ יִכְבֹּשׁ עֲוֹנֹתֵינוּ וְתַשְׁלִיךְ בִּמְצֻלוֹת יָם כָּל־חַטֹּאתָם
languages.english:
Who is a God like You, who pardons iniquity and forgives transgression
For the remnant of His heritage?
He does not maintain His anger forever,
For He delights in mercy.
He will again have compassion on us,
And will subdue our iniquities.
You will cast all our sins into the depths of the sea.

prayers.meaning: A prayer of repentance recited on Rosh Hashanah, symbolically casting sins into flowing water.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Tashlich ('casting off') represents the desire to start fresh. The flowing water symbolizes God's mercy carrying away sins. It reflects the Jewish belief in the possibility of genuine return (teshuvah).

prayers.title: Kaddish (The Mourner's Prayer)

languages.aramaic:
יִתְגַּדַּל וְיִתְקַדַּשׁ שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא
בְּעָלְמָא דִּי בְרָא כִרְעוּתֵהּ
וְיַמְלִיךְ מַלְכוּתֵהּ בְּחַיֵּיכוֹן וּבְיוֹמֵיכוֹן
וּבְחַיֵּי דְכָל בֵּית יִשְׂרָאֵל
בַּעֲגָלָא וּבִזְמַן קָרִיב
וְאִמְרוּ אָמֵן
languages.transliteration:
Yitgadal v'yitkadash sh'mei raba
B'alma di v'ra chirutei
V'yamlich malchutei b'chayeichon uv'yomeichon
Uv'chayei d'chol beit Yisrael
Ba'agala uvizman kariv
V'imru amen
languages.english:
Exalted and hallowed be His great Name
In the world which He created according to His will.
May He establish His kingdom during your lifetime and during your days,
And during the lifetimes of all the House of Israel,
Speedily and in the near future,
And say, Amen.

prayers.meaning: A prayer sanctifying God's name, traditionally recited by mourners. Despite being associated with death, it never mentions death - only affirms life and God's greatness.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The Kaddish is an act of faith in the face of loss. By praising God even during mourning, the bereaved affirms that God's plan is greater than human understanding.

prayers.title: Surah Al-Ikhlas (The Purity/Sincerity)

languages.arabic:
قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ
اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ
لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ
وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌ
languages.transliteration:
Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad
Allahu Samad
Lam yalid wa lam yulad
Wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan ahad
languages.english:
Say: He is Allah, the One.
Allah, the Eternal, Absolute.
He begets not, nor is He begotten.
And there is none comparable unto Him.

prayers.meaning: A complete declaration of God's absolute unity and uniqueness, negating any partners, offspring, or equals.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) said this surah equals one-third of the Quran because it contains the essence of Tawhid (Divine Unity). Its recitation purifies faith from all forms of shirk (polytheism).

prayers.title: Surah An-Nas (Mankind) - Protection Prayer

languages.arabic:
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ
مَلِكِ النَّاسِ
إِلَٰهِ النَّاسِ
مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ
الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ
مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ
languages.transliteration:
Qul a'udhu bi Rabbin-nas
Malikin-nas
Ilahin-nas
Min sharril waswaasil khannas
Alladhi yuwaswisu fi sudoorin-nas
Minal jinnati wannas
languages.english:
Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind,
The King of mankind,
The God of mankind,
From the evil of the retreating whisperer,
Who whispers in the breasts of mankind,
From among jinn and mankind.

prayers.meaning: A prayer seeking divine protection from the evil whispers that cause doubt, fear, and spiritual harm.
prayers.spiritual_significance: One of the two 'protection surahs' (with Al-Falaq), recited for protection from spiritual and psychological harm. The three divine attributes (Lord, King, God) represent complete sovereignty over all creation.

prayers.title: Prayer of St. Francis
prayers.origin: Attributed to St. Francis of Assisi (c. 1181-1226)
prayers.meaning: A prayer of self-surrender seeking to be a channel of divine peace, love, and healing to others.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This prayer embodies the paradoxes of the spiritual life: receiving through giving, healing through forgiving, living through dying to self. It represents the highest call to selfless service.

prayers.title: Serenity Prayer

languages.english:
God, grant me the serenity to accept the things I cannot change,
The courage to change the things I can,
And the wisdom to know the difference.

Living one day at a time,
Enjoying one moment at a time,
Accepting hardship as a pathway to peace,
Taking, as Jesus did, this sinful world as it is,
Not as I would have it,
Trusting that You will make all things right
If I surrender to Your will,
So that I may be reasonably happy in this life
And supremely happy with You forever in the next.
languages.shona:
Mwari, ndipei rugare rwekugamuchira zvinhu zvandisingagoni kushandura,
Ushingi hwekushandura zvinhu zvandinogona,
Nouchenjeri hwekuziva musiyano.

Kurarama zuva rimwe chete panguva,
Kufadzwa nenguva imwe chete panguva,
Kugamuchira kutambudzika senzira yerugare,
Kutora, sezvakaita Jesu, nyika ino yechivi sezvairi,
Kwete sezvandaida kuti ive,
Ndichivimba kuti muchaita zvinhu zvose zvakarurama
Kana ndikakuzviisai kuda kwenyu,
Kuti ndigone kuva wakafara zvakakwana muupenyu uhwu
Uye wakafara kwazvo nemi nokusingaperi mune unotevera.

prayers.meaning: A prayer for peace, courage, and wisdom in facing life's challenges, distinguishing between what can and cannot be changed.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Widely adopted by recovery movements, this prayer teaches radical acceptance combined with responsible action. It embodies the balance between surrender and effort in spiritual growth.

prayers.title: Chinamoto chaBaba Johani (Prayer of Baba Johani)

languages.shona:
Mwari Baba, Mutongi weZuva neUsiku,
Mukuru weMweya, Musiki weZvose,
Ndinouya pamberi penyu namanheru ano,
Mwoyo wangu wakashama senyika yaoma.
Ndidzoserei kumvura yeupenyu,
Ndiregererei zvatakakutadzirai,
Ndichengetedzei kubva kumweya yakaipa.

Nemi makatuma Mweya Mutsvene,
Mweya unotaura nenzwi risinganzwiki,
Mweya unoratidza zvisingaonekwi.
Ndizadzai neMweya wenyu,
Kuti ndifambe munzira yechokwadi,
Nzira yaMose naEria,
Nzira yaIshe wedu Jesu Kristu.

Pasi pose pauri, Baba,
Kudenga nepasirese pauri,
Makomo nemvura zvakateerera izwi renyu.
Neniwo ndinoteerera.
Ndinogamuchira kuda kwenyu.
Ameni.
languages.english:
God the Father, Judge of Day and Night,
Great One of the Spirit, Creator of All,
I come before You this evening,
My heart is open like dry land.
Restore me to the waters of life,
Forgive us where we have wronged You,
Protect me from evil spirits.

You who sent the Holy Spirit,
The Spirit that speaks with an inaudible voice,
The Spirit that shows what is unseen.
Fill me with Your Spirit,
That I may walk in the way of truth,
The way of Moses and Elijah,
The way of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Everywhere on earth You are, Father,
Heaven and earth are under You,
Mountains and waters obey Your voice.
I too obey.
I accept Your will.
Amen.

prayers.meaning: A prayer invoking God's restoration, forgiveness, and protection, acknowledging the Holy Spirit's guidance in the tradition of biblical prophets.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Baba Johani Masowe founded the African Apostolic church movement, blending biblical faith with African spiritual practice. This prayer reflects the emphasis on Spirit-led living, prophetic vision, and harmony with creation.

prayers.title: Spell for Coming Forth by Day
prayers.taught_by: Priests of the temple of Ra

languages.english:
I am Yesterday, Today, and Tomorrow,
And I have the power to be born a second time.
I am the divine hidden Soul
Who creates the gods and gives sepulchral meals
To the denizens of the Duat, Amentet, and heaven.

I am the Rudder of the East, the possessor of two divine faces
Wherein the beams are seen.
I am the Lord of those who are raised up from the dead,
The Lord who comes forth from the darkness.

Open for me, O ye who guard the gates!
The earth opens for me, Geb, Lord of the gods, has opened his jaws;
My eyes which were closed are opened,
My limbs which were bound are released.

Thoth has come fully equipped with words of power.
I am Horus, the elder son of Osiris.
I am Yesterday, and I know Tomorrow!

prayers.meaning: A spell enabling the deceased to move freely between the world of the living and the realm of the dead, 'coming forth by day' to revisit the earth.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This spell represents the ultimate victory over death - the ability to exist in both worlds freely. 'Coming forth by day' is the Egyptian term for resurrection, the title of the entire Book of the Dead.

prayers.title: Prayer of Transformation into the Golden Falcon

languages.english:
I have risen! I have gathered myself together like the beautiful golden falcon
Which has the head of a benu-bird.
Ra enters in to hear my words.
I have settled myself among the great gods
Who dwell in the Field of Offerings.

I seize the moment; I fly as a falcon!
I scream as the goose Geb.
I fly and alight on the path of the pure spirits
At the great festival.

What I abominate, I shall not eat.
What I abominate is excrement; I shall not eat it.
What I abominate is filth of every kind; I shall not eat it.
Not shall it come to my hands, nor shall I step upon it with my sandals.

I am purified! I am transformed!
I have become the golden falcon,
Born of the great mother,
Son of the morning sun!

prayers.meaning: A transformation spell allowing the soul to take the form of a divine golden falcon, symbol of Horus and royal power.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The falcon represented the soul's ability to soar above earthly limitations. This transformation granted the deceased divine power and the ability to travel freely through all realms.

prayers.title: The Ba Soul Prayer

languages.english:
O you soul-bringers who bring souls,
Who build up souls, who give souls,
Who are over the created things of Ra,
Who are established on your places,
Who make your speech firm!

May my Ba soul come to me from wherever it may be.
If there be delay in bringing my Ba soul to me,
You will find the Eye of Horus standing up against you,
Like those watchers who watch the eye.

My Ba has come back to me;
My shade has come back to me;
I am equipped as a living Ba!
I am a Ba that is greater than all other Ba souls.
My Ba lives!
My spirit lives!
I am living forever!

prayers.meaning: A prayer for the reunion of the Ba soul with the body, essential for resurrection and continued existence after death.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The Egyptians believed the Ba (personality/soul) separated from the body at death but needed to reunite for resurrection. This prayer ensures the soul's return, making eternal life possible.

prayers.title: Prayer at the Fields of Hetep (Paradise)

languages.english:
I know the Field of Reeds of Ra.
The height of its barley is four cubits,
Its ear is one cubit, its stalk is three cubits.
Its emmer wheat is seven cubits,
Its ear is two cubits, its stalk is five cubits.

It is the spirits, each nine cubits in their height,
Who reap it by the side of the Eastern Souls.
I know the Eastern Souls: they are Horakhty, the Khepri-Calf,
And the Morning Star.

I have come into the Fields of Hetep,
The garden of the great god.
I breathe the wind of the north among the palm trees.
I eat bread of the Imperishable Stars.
I drink water from the cool oasis.

I am at peace in this beautiful place.
My offerings shall not fail.
My spirit shall not hunger.
I shall live forever in the presence of the Great Ones!

prayers.meaning: A vision of paradise - the Field of Reeds (Sekhet Aaru) where the blessed dead live in eternal abundance, performing no labor but enjoying endless provision.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The Field of Hetep (Peace/Offerings) represents the Egyptian concept of heaven - an idealized Egypt with abundant harvests, cool breezes, and the company of the blessed. It is the reward for living according to Ma'at.

prayers.title: Prayer Against the Second Death

languages.english:
O Thoth, what is it that has happened to the divine children?
They have stirred up strife, they have done wrong,
They have created rebellion, they have made slaughter,
They have caused trouble: truly in all their doings
The mighty have worked against the weak.

O Thoth, grant that I may not look upon the second death.
I shall not die a second time.
I have made my place far from all who would harm me.
The crocodile shall not seize me,
The serpent shall not strike me,
No foe shall approach me.

For I am one who knows the mystery!
I am one who has passed through the gate!
I am one who has eaten of the tree!
I shall not die the death of the second death!
I am established forever in the fields of Ra!
My name shall not be destroyed!
I shall live, I shall live!
I shall not die, I shall not die!

prayers.meaning: A prayer for protection from the 'second death' - complete spiritual annihilation - which was the ultimate fear of the Egyptians.
prayers.spiritual_significance: The 'second death' meant the total destruction of the soul, erasure from existence. This prayer invokes protection from this fate through knowledge of divine mysteries and passage through sacred gates.

prayers.title: Modeh Ani (Morning Gratitude Prayer)

languages.hebrew: מוֹדֶה אֲנִי לְפָנֶיךָ מֶלֶךְ חַי וְקַיָּם שֶׁהֶחֱזַרְתָּ בִּי נִשְׁמָתִי בְּחֶמְלָה רַבָּה אֱמוּנָתֶךָ
languages.transliteration: Modeh ani l'fanecha, Melech chai v'kayam, shehechezarta bi nishmati b'chemla, raba emunatecha.

prayers.meaning: A prayer of gratitude recited immediately upon waking, thanking God for restoring the soul after the 'little death' of sleep.
prayers.spiritual_significance: In Jewish thought, sleep is compared to death, and waking to resurrection. This prayer begins the day with gratitude, recognizing that each new day is a divine gift.

prayers.title: Adon Olam (Lord of the Universe)
prayers.taught_by: Attributed to Solomon ibn Gabirol (11th century)

languages.hebrew:
אֲדוֹן עוֹלָם אֲשֶׁר מָלַךְ בְּטֶרֶם כָּל יְצִיר נִבְרָא
לְעֵת נַעֲשָׂה בְחֶפְצוֹ כֹּל אֲזַי מֶלֶךְ שְׁמוֹ נִקְרָא
וְאַחֲרֵי כִּכְלוֹת הַכֹּל לְבַדּוֹ יִמְלוֹךְ נוֹרָא
וְהוּא הָיָה וְהוּא הֹוֶה וְהוּא יִהְיֶה בְּתִפְאָרָה
languages.transliteration:
Adon olam asher malach, b'terem kol y'tzir nivra.
L'et na'asah v'cheftzo kol, azai melech sh'mo nikra.
V'acharei kichlot hakol, l'vado yimloch nora.
V'hu haya, v'hu hoveh, v'hu yih'yeh b'tifara.
languages.english:
Lord of the universe who reigned before any creature was created.
When all was made by His will, then was His name proclaimed King.
And after all shall cease to be, alone He will reign in awe.
He was, He is, and He shall be in glory.

prayers.meaning: A hymn declaring God's eternal sovereignty - before creation, during existence, and after all ends.
prayers.spiritual_significance: This prayer encompasses all of time and existence, declaring God's eternal nature. It is sung both at the beginning and end of services, framing worship in the context of eternity.

prayers.title: Salat al-Istikhara (Guidance Prayer)

languages.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْتَخِيرُكَ بِعِلْمِكَ وَأَسْتَقْدِرُكَ بِقُدْرَتِكَ وَأَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْعَظِيمِ فَإِنَّكَ تَقْدِرُ وَلَا أَقْدِرُ وَتَعْلَمُ وَلَا أَعْلَمُ وَأَنْتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ اللَّهُمَّ إِنْ كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ هَذَا الْأَمْرَ خَيْرٌ لِي فِي دِينِي وَمَعَاشِي وَعَاقِبَةِ أَمْرِي فَاقْدُرْهُ لِي وَيَسِّرْهُ لِي ثُمَّ بَارِكْ لِي فِيهِ وَإِنْ كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ هَذَا الْأَمْرَ شَرٌّ لِي فِي دِينِي وَمَعَاشِي وَعَاقِبَةِ أَمْرِي فَاصْرِفْهُ عَنِّي وَاصْرِفْنِي عَنْهُ وَاقْدُرْ لِيَ الْخَيْرَ حَيْثُ كَانَ ثُمَّ أَرْضِنِي بِهِ
languages.english:
O Allah, I seek Your guidance by virtue of Your knowledge,
And I seek ability by virtue of Your power,
And I ask You of Your great bounty.
You have power; I have none.
And You know; I know not.
You are the Knower of hidden things.

O Allah, if in Your knowledge this matter is good for me
In my religion, my livelihood, and my affairs,
Then ordain it for me, make it easy for me, and bless it for me.
And if in Your knowledge this matter is bad for me
In my religion, my livelihood, and my affairs,
Then turn it away from me, and turn me away from it,
And ordain for me the good wherever it may be,
And make me content with it.

prayers.meaning: A prayer seeking divine guidance when facing important decisions, trusting Allah's knowledge over human understanding.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Istikhara represents complete trust (tawakkul) in Allah. Rather than relying on human wisdom alone, the believer seeks divine direction, accepting that Allah knows what is best.

prayers.title: Du'a for Parents

languages.english: My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up when I was small.

prayers.meaning: A prayer asking God to show mercy to one's parents in return for their care during one's childhood.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Honoring parents is among the highest duties in Islam, mentioned immediately after worship of Allah. This prayer is a daily expression of gratitude and intercession for parents.

prayers.title: Prayer of Manasseh
prayers.taught_by: Attributed to King Manasseh during captivity

languages.english:
O Lord Almighty, God of our ancestors,
Of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob and of their righteous offspring;
You who made heaven and earth with all their order;
Who bound the sea by Your word of command,
Who confined the deep and sealed it with Your terrible and glorious name.

I have sinned, O Lord, I have sinned,
And I know my transgressions.
I earnestly implore You: Forgive me, O Lord, forgive me!
Do not destroy me with my transgressions!

For You, O Lord, are the God of those who repent,
And in me You will manifest Your goodness;
For, unworthy as I am, You will save me according to Your great mercy,
And I will praise You continually all the days of my life.
For all the host of heaven sings Your praise,
And Yours is the glory forever. Amen.

prayers.meaning: A prayer of deep repentance from one who committed great sins but sought God's mercy.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Manasseh was the most wicked king of Judah, yet according to tradition he repented while in captivity. This prayer shows that no sin is beyond God's forgiveness for the truly penitent.

prayers.title: Om Mani Padme Hum

languages.english: Om, the Jewel in the Lotus, Hum

prayers.meaning: A mantra invoking the blessing of Chenrezig (Avalokiteshvara), embodying compassion for all beings.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Each syllable purifies one of the six realms of existence and represents a different aspect of the path to enlightenment. It is the most widely used mantra in Tibetan Buddhism, generating compassion.

prayers.title: Namo Amituofo (Homage to Amitabha Buddha)

languages.english: Homage to the Buddha of Infinite Light

prayers.meaning: A devotional phrase expressing faith in Amitabha Buddha and aspiration to be reborn in his Pure Land.
prayers.spiritual_significance: In Pure Land Buddhism, sincere recitation of this name ensures rebirth in Amitabha's Western Paradise, where conditions are ideal for achieving enlightenment. It represents the 'easy path' to liberation.

prayers.title: Munamato weKutenda (Prayer of Thanksgiving)

languages.shona:
Maita Mwari Baba,
Maita Musikavanhu,
Maita Nyadenga,
Maita Samasimba.

Tinokutendai nezvose zvamakatiitira:
Nemvura yekumwa neyekudiridza,
Nezviyo zvinokura muminda,
Nemhuri yedu nenyika yedu.

Vadzimu vedu, madzibaba namadzimbuya,
Tinokutendai nokutichengeta,
Nokutitungamirira munzira dzeupenyu,
Nokutipa njere nokuchenjera.

Ngakuve neMwari nekusingaperi.
Ameni.
languages.english:
Thank You, God the Father,
Thank You, Creator of Humanity,
Thank You, Great One of Heaven,
Thank You, Almighty.

We thank You for all You have done for us:
For water to drink and to irrigate,
For grain that grows in the fields,
For our family and our land.

Our ancestors, fathers and grandmothers,
We thank you for protecting us,
For guiding us in the ways of life,
For giving us wisdom and understanding.

May it be with God forever.
Amen.

prayers.meaning: A Shona prayer of thanksgiving honoring both God (Mwari) and the ancestors who serve as intermediaries between the living and the divine.
prayers.spiritual_significance: Traditional Shona spirituality recognizes Mwari as the supreme creator while honoring ancestors (vadzimu) as spiritual guides. This prayer reflects the African worldview of interconnection between the living, the dead, and the divine.
